channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Foxes causing an issue., 29th March
item.description:
The garden which belongs to the council is right next to my back garden. The foxes are causing an issue and also scaring my pets away. There has been a few occasions our furniture/personal belongings was damaged by the foxes. There are minimum 3 foxes
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hillstowe Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Garden flight tippers, 29th March
item.description:
Well, the Green fingered fly chippers are back again

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Sandhurst Road, Edmonton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Illuminated sign - out, 29th March
item.description:
Ethelbert rd On street light E29 2
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 84, High Street, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pot holes and Uneven surfaces, 29th March
item.description:
I regularly drive along Summers Lane to and from work. The road surface is extremely poor with many uneven levels and pot holes. This is extremely dangerous to road users particularly cyclists and motorcyclists. The issue has been there for some time. When are you going to fix it?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 41, Summers Lane, Fallow Corner, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Recent repair of poor quality, 29th March
item.description:
Hi, Recent the pedestrian strip in this areas was patched. I assume to fix the loose paving which is very hazardous. However the repair has all broken up and not even fixed the issue. Please can this be fixed asap as now there is loose tarmac everywhere Many Thanks Ian Gartrell

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Jenner Close, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Multiple pot holes, 29th March
item.description:
I regularly drive down this road on my way to and from work. The state of the road is shocking! I recently went over a pot hole and this damaged a tyre sensor in my car. Since then all the electronics on my vehicle are faulty. I do not have the money to carry out a repair. Who will take responsibility for this road? I know the pot hole was the cause as immediately after electronic symbols and warning lights appeared on the dash board. These pot holes have been there for some time. They are impossible to avoid due to the position they are in.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 60, The Bishops Avenue, Hampstead Garden Suburb, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Vegetation overtaking light, 29th March
item.description:
Please can the vegetation be removed from the light. Many thanks

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 14, Swallowtail Close, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Several Pots Holes, 29th March
item.description:
There are several pot holes along this road which are getting bigger, and can feel the houses are vibration as traffic passes. I have been asked to report this on behalf of neighbours
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Prospect Vale, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter pick needed, 29th March
item.description:
Please can a litter pick be carried our on Buttermere Road, Wagtail Way and Teal Avenue. There is the start of a build up of rubbish being blown around. Many thanks

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, Buttermere Road, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Can the mayor of Hackney please explain what their policy is of misuse of drugs in public parks, public areas in hackney. There is a high level of misuse of drugs in public parks on a daily basis where families and children spend time.what is the mayor doing this anti-social behaviour.., 29th March
item.description:
High levels of drugs being used in public parks and public areas in Hackney. Can the mayor please address these issues. No enforcement. No policing. possibly corruption.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Mare Street, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Kitchen appliance flytipped, 29th March
item.description:
Please check and remove. Many thanks

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 31, Wagtail Way, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken paving slabs, 29th March
item.description:
Dear Fix My Street, There are 3 cracked paving slabs on the pavement in front of 33 Broadcroft Road, Petts Wood, BR5 1ET. Please see picture uploaded. One is a particular trip hazard as it rocks. Please can they be replaced? The next door neighbour on the other side of the shared drive (on the left of 33 viewed from front) is having a ramp onto their new printed concrete drive (visible in the uploaded picture). Perhaps, some of the good slabs from here can be used in front of 33. Hope this ok? John

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 40, Broadcroft Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Debris and leaves cluttering the street, 29th March
item.description:
As above. Street cleaning required.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cedar Copse, Bickley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: POst litter pick bins, 29th March
item.description:
Hello, Friends of Harvington did a litter pick on Friday. Would it be possible to collect the refuse we picked up please. Thanks Friends of Harvington

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): South Eden Park Road, Eden Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Line bike causing anti-social behavior and nuisance to members are public and residents on a daily basis after moving these bikes out the way or off of people's cars. Hackney Council only care about investment and money that they are receiving from line bikes. Line bikes are a inconvenience for many people causing upset and obstructions in the areaYes, Hackney Council receives funds from a new agreement with e-bike operators Lime and Voi, from which it will receive a cut of the income. The council also partners with other organizations and initiatives to provide and fund various hire schemes, such as the "Cargo Bike Share" scheme, which is part of a wider network of shared bikes, and has secured funding through grants from the government for a "Cycle Loan" program for its employees. ., 29th March
item.description:
What is the mayor of Hackney doing about these antisocial behavior line bikes on our streets causing hazards, obstructions, danger to pedestrians and motor vehicles bikes lying around all over the place very dangerous can cause a car collision.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Semley Gate, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Several deep and long potholes in middle of the road, 29th March
item.description:
Junction Crawford Gardens and Farndale Avenue N13.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Farndale Avenue, Southgate
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping n this van, 29th March
item.description:
This van fly tipping

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Eastney Street, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dangerous unsheathed needles, 29th March
item.description:
Unsheathed heroin needles are causing significant risk outside our property. Additionally the drug users are causing a disturbance to our household.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): New North Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown back garden causing fox issues, 29th March
item.description:
This garden (owned by the council), has clearly not been used in years. It has become a dumping ground and is so overgrown that there’s now a fox den inside. This is a enviromental safety issue as now there are packs of foxes around all the gardens.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hillstowe Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Alot of Stella cans, 29th March
item.description:
Right on the corner of alleyn park and acacia drive many cans of Stella has been chucked

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Alleyn Park, Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
